title: How Much Does It Cost to Start a Restaurant in Massachusetts?
content:
Starting a Restaurant in Massachusetts typically costs between $220,000 and $943,000. That is about 26% higher than the national average of $175,000-$750,000, driven primarily by Massachusetts's above-average cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

Massachusetts offers access to a highly educated workforce, world-class universities, and a strong innovation economy centered around Boston and Cambridge. The trade-off is high costs across the board - the $500 LLC filing and $500 annual report fee are among the highest in the nation. The state's new 4% surtax on income over $1 million adds to the tax burden for successful businesses.

From a regulatory standpoint, Massachusetts has specific requirements to plan for. Massachusetts's top income tax rate of 9% is among the highest in the nation, which will take a meaningful bite out of profits as your business grows. The state minimum wage of $15/hour is well above the federal level, which pushes labor costs higher for businesses that rely on hourly employees.

Cost Breakdown for Massachusetts

Costs adjusted for Massachusetts's cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

Massachusetts Business Requirements

To legally operate a restaurant in Massachusetts, you will need to:

  • Form an LLC or business entity - The filing fee in Massachusetts is $500, with a $500 annual report fee.
  • Obtain a business license - Requirements and fees vary by city. Contact your local Boston or Worcester clerk's office for specifics.
  • Register for sales tax - Massachusetts's state sales tax rate is 6.3%. Local additions can push the effective rate higher. You will need a sales tax permit if you sell taxable goods or services.
  • Plan for state income tax - Massachusetts's top rate is 9%. Set aside a portion of profits for quarterly estimated payments.
  • Get business insurance - General liability insurance is essential in Massachusetts. Most landlords and clients require at least $1 million in coverage.

Why Massachusetts Costs Differ

Massachusetts's cost of living is 25% above the national average, which affects everything from supplies to services you need to purchase. Labor costs run about 20% above average, driven by a $15/hour minimum wage and market competition for workers in Boston and surrounding areas. Commercial rent is the biggest cost driver in Massachusetts - expect to pay 35% more than the national average for retail or commercial space, particularly in Boston.

Massachusetts-Specific Tips

  • In Massachusetts's high-cost market, consider starting lean - test your concept at a smaller scale before signing long-term leases or making big equipment purchases.
  • Budget $500 for the LLC filing fee in Massachusetts, which is above the national average. Consider whether an LLC is necessary right away or if you can operate as a sole proprietorship initially.
  • Don't forget Massachusetts's $500 annual report fee for LLCs - it's an ongoing cost that catches new business owners off guard.
  • Commercial kitchen space in Massachusetts runs above the national average. Look for second-generation restaurant space (previously a restaurant) to save on buildout costs - the plumbing, ventilation, and grease traps may already be in place.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to start a restaurant in Massachusetts?

Starting a restaurant in Massachusetts typically costs between $220,000 and $943,000, depending on your location within the state, your business model, and how lean you launch. Costs in Boston tend to run higher than in smaller Massachusetts cities like Cambridge.

Do I need a special license to operate a restaurant in Massachusetts?

Yes. At minimum, you need a Massachusetts business license and any industry-specific permits required by your city or county. LLC formation costs $500 in Massachusetts, plus a $500 annual report fee. Check with your local Boston or Worcester city hall for specific requirements.

How does Massachusetts's state income tax affect my restaurant?

Massachusetts's top state income tax rate is 9%. As a business owner, you will typically pay this on your business profits through your personal return (if you are an LLC or sole proprietorship). Factor this into your pricing and profitability projections from the start.

Is Massachusetts a good state to start a restaurant?

Massachusetts has above-average costs but also a large consumer market. Massachusetts offers access to a highly educated workforce, world-class universities, and a strong innovation economy centered around Boston and Cambridge.

See our full national Restaurant cost guide for detailed breakdowns, hidden costs, and money-saving strategies that apply everywhere.
